title: Stage script env contract required — no stable calling convention defined
body: All four agents independently identified that stage scripts (spec-review.sh, plan.sh, etc.) have no defined calling convention. Without a standard env block (SLUG, QUEUE_DIR, ARTIFACT_DIR, CONFIG_FILE), run.sh and stage scripts are coupled at the source level and individually untestable.

title: Stage 2 context handoff mechanism — predecessor-artifact convention chosen
body: All three agents that flagged the unresolved handoff converged on the same solution: each stage script reads its predecessor's artifact from queue/<slug>/ by convention. This is cleaner than prompt injection (no context-window growth) and mirrors the interactive pipeline's artifact consumption pattern.

title: Command file blocking language is the #1 integration risk — approval gates will stall headless runs
body: spec-review.md, plan.md, and build.md contain hard-wired approval gates ('Approve to proceed?', 'Launch Wave 1?', 'Justin controls pace'). A headless claude -p subprocess will emit these and wait for stdin. The autonomy directive may not reliably suppress them. The spike must test this as item 2.

title: In-progress sentinel missing — crashed runs undetectable on re-run
body: All three agents independently identified the same gap: no in-progress state sentinel means a crashed run leaves a slug directory that looks neither complete nor failed. Design decision: state.json (machine-readable, owned by run.sh) + queue/.current-stage (human-readable, updated each stage).

title: failure.md schema undefined — operator cannot diagnose or recover without guessing
body: Three agents converged on the same gap: failure.md exists in the spec but has no schema. The recommendation is a structured template with: stage, wave, branch name, pre-build SHA, retry count, error tail, and a literal re-queue command. This is also the primary input to notify.sh.

title: claude -p parallelism in headless mode unverified — 6 sequential reviewers would be 6x slower
body: Both agents flagged that claude -p is a blocking synchronous call. Whether it honors Agent-tool parallelism inside headless invocations is unverified. If sequential, spec-review stage takes ~12 min vs ~2 min parallel. Still overnight-viable but time estimate is wrong. This is the spike's most critical test item (item 1).

title: autorun wrapper script (start/stop/status) — high effort-to-UX-gain ratio
body: A thin shell wrapper that exposes 'autorun start|stop|status' solves invocation verbosity, kill-switch discoverability, and monitoring in one shot. Without it, the day-one experience requires remembering 'flock -n queue/.autorun.lock bash scripts/autorun/run.sh' at 10pm.

title: autorun.config.json must be gitignored — webhook_url leaks if committed to public repo
body: autorun.config.json contains webhook_url (Slack/Discord incoming webhook). If committed and repo is ever public, the webhook is permanently exposed. Trivial to fix with .gitignore. Provide autorun.config.json.example instead.

title: install.sh flat-glob misses scripts/autorun/ subdirectory — wrappers won't be symlinked
body: install.sh currently globs scripts/*.sh (flat). scripts/autorun/*.sh will not be symlinked without a subdirectory loop. Every autorun script also needs +x set on the source file before install.

title: Per-call timeout required — hung claude -p blocks entire queue indefinitely
body: Without timeout on each claude -p invocation, a hung process (network timeout, process not exiting) blocks the entire queue until manually killed. 'timeout 300 claude -p ...' is a 1-line fix per invocation.

title: queue/index.md and structured run log for morning debrief and forensic audit trail
body: Two convergent recommendations: UX wants queue/index.md (slug|verdict|stage|PR URL written at run end); Security wants queue/run.log (JSON lines per wave, gitignored). Together these replace the need for human gate before auto-merge — they are the forensic trail.

title: AUTORUN_GH_TOKEN — scoped PAT limits blast radius if token exfiltrated via prompt injection
body: GH_TOKEN is live in every subprocess. A scoped fine-grained PAT limited to autorun/* branches + create/merge PRs on the specific repo limits damage if token is exfiltrated via a malicious spec file.
